name: BAASS Full
description: The "BAASS Full" theory, primarily circulating within online UFO and conspiracy communities, posits that the Pentagon's Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP), later succeeded by the Unidentified Aerial Phenomena Task Force (UAPTF) and then the All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO), only represents a small fraction of a much larger, more secretive, and potentially malevolent government operation known as "BAASS Full." Proponents believe this larger operation is deeply embedded within the military-industrial complex and actively suppresses information regarding extraterrestrial contact and advanced technologies derived from crashed UFOs. The term "BAASS" is often linked to Bigelow Aerospace Advanced Space Studies, a company that worked with AATIP. "BAASS Full" suggests a conspiracy to hoard these technologies for military advantage and control, preventing their release to the public. Evidence cited includes leaked documents, whistleblower testimonies (often anonymous or unverifiable), and interpretations of government actions as deliberate disinformation campaigns. Mainstream perspectives view these claims as unsubstantiated, based on speculation and misinterpretations of publicly available information or declassified documents. Skeptics argue that alleged evidence lacks verifiable proof and relies heavily on conjecture and confirmation bias. Government transparency efforts regarding UAPs are often cited as evidence against a large-scale cover-up, though critics argue these efforts are merely controlled releases of information designed to maintain secrecy. The theory thrives on distrust of government institutions and the allure of hidden knowledge.
subtitle: Deep State UFO Cover-Up Conspiracy

claims.claim: BAASS Full is a larger, more secretive government program than AATIP/UAPTF/AARO.
claims.evidence: Anonymous whistleblower testimonies, circumstantial evidence from leaked documents, and interpretations of government secrecy.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable evidence, reliance on unverifiable sources, and misinterpretation of government procedures.

claims.claim: BAASS Full actively suppresses information about extraterrestrial contact and advanced technologies.
claims.evidence: Alleged disinformation campaigns, withholding of information from the public, and intimidation of witnesses.
claims.criticism: Government transparency efforts regarding UAPs, lack of concrete proof of suppression, and alternative explanations for government secrecy.

claims.claim: BAASS Full hoards recovered UFO technology for military advantage and control.
claims.evidence: Speculation based on the military-industrial complex, claims of reverse-engineered technologies, and theories about secret military programs.
claims.criticism: Lack of physical evidence of recovered UFOs, alternative explanations for advanced military technologies, and the infeasibility of maintaining such a large-scale secret.

keyFigures.name: Hal Puthoff
keyFigures.role: Scientist involved with BAASS and AATIP, often cited by proponents.

keyFigures.name: Mick West
keyFigures.role: Prominent skeptic and debunker of UFO claims.

timeline.event: New York Times article reveals AATIP existence, fueling speculation.

sources.author: Leslie Kean
sources.work: UFOs: Generals, Pilots, and Government Officials Go on the Record
sources.title: Leslie Kean

sources.author: George Knapp
sources.work: Various articles and documentaries on UFOs
sources.title: George Knapp

variants: Bigelow Aerospace Advanced Space Studies Full
